summary: express-session-kit typosquats express-session and fetches remote JavaScript to execute via new Function at load time
details: express-session-kit@1.1.1 impersonates the popular express-session middleware, copying its source verbatim and declaring the real express-session author and an 'expressjs/session-kit' repository to appear official. Alongside the copied middleware it ships an added payload: index.js spawns a detached node child at module load to execute session/check.js, which POSTs to http://check-server-state.vercel.app/server/v2 over plain HTTP and, on a 404 response carrying a 'token' field, passes that value to `new Function('require', <body>)` and invokes it — running attacker-supplied JavaScript inside the installer's Node process with full require() access. Evasion signals include a misdirected loader path ('./lib/check.js' vs the actual './session/check.js'), innocuous naming ('check-server-state', header 'bearrtoken', token literal 'gemini'), and detached/stdio-ignored child_process to hide the beacon. Any application that require()s this package hands the maintainer of the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution on the installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
